raw_response:
I agree with the chosen option A (20-40 μ). Maintaining a thickness within this range optimally balances mechanical strength required for successful adhesion without compromising the integrity of the underlying tooth structure or causing excessive thermal irritation to the pulp tissue. Any deviation outside this narrow band could either compromise bond quality or introduce unacceptable levels of thermal sensitivity, thereby increasing potential complications during and after restorative procedures. Therefore, my final answer remains consistent with Option A. 

Answer Choice: A (20-40 μ).
